domain: Solar System Astrophysics and Planetary Evolution

entities.name: Solar System
entities.description: The gravitationally bound planetary system consisting of the Sun and all the objects that orbit it, formed approximately 4.57 billion years ago.

entities.name: Sun
entities.description: The main-sequence star located at the center of the Solar System, composed primarily of hydrogen and helium, which generates energy via nuclear fusion.

entities.name: Mercury
entities.description: The innermost rocky planet in the Solar System, characterized by its lack of natural satellites and highly cratered surface.

entities.name: Venus
entities.description: The second planet from the Sun, featuring a dense atmosphere, extreme greenhouse heating, and no moons.

entities.name: Earth
entities.description: The third planet from the Sun, characterized by its dynamic geological activity, surface water, and one natural satellite.

entities.name: Mars
entities.description: The fourth rocky planet from the Sun, containing a thin atmosphere, surface craters, and two small natural satellites.

entities.name: Jupiter
entities.description: The largest planet in the Solar System, a gas giant primarily composed of hydrogen and helium with dozens of natural satellites.

entities.name: Saturn
entities.description: The second-largest planet in the Solar System, celebrated for its extensive planetary ring system and numerous moons.

entities.name: Uranus
entities.description: An outer outer planet categorized as an ice giant, containing a highly tilted rotation axis and a system of moons.

entities.name: Neptune
entities.description: The farthest major planet from the Sun, classified as an ice giant with strong winds and active atmospheric patterns.

entities.name: Protoplanetary Disk
entities.description: A circumstellar disk of dense gas and dust surrounding a newly formed protostar, serving as the birthplace of planets.

entities.name: Planetesimal
entities.description: Solid objects created through the collision and accretion of dust grains within a protoplanetary disk.

entities.name: Protoplanet
entities.description: Large planetary embryos generated from the merging of planetesimals that undergo internal differentiation.

entities.name: Main Asteroid Belt
entities.description: A circumstellar region located between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter occupied by numerous small, irregularly shaped rocky objects.

entities.name: Kuiper Belt
entities.description: A vast, torus-shaped debris disk in the outer Solar System extending past the orbit of Neptune, hosting cold, icy objects and dwarf planets.

entities.name: Oort Cloud
entities.description: An immense spherical cloud of icy bodies proposed to surround the Sun at the furthest margins of its gravitational influence.

entities.name: Pluto
entities.description: A icy dwarf planet and prominent plutoid located within the Kuiper Belt, orbited by five known moons.

entities.name: Ceres
entities.description: The largest object situated in the main asteroid belt, classified as a dwarf planet due to its spherical shape.

entities.name: Eris
entities.description: A highly massive trans-Neptunian plutoid and dwarf planet located in the outer solar system.

entities.name: Haumea
entities.description: An elongated dwarf planet and official plutoid orbiting within the Kuiper Belt.

entities.name: Makemake
entities.description: A major Kuiper Belt object classified as an official plutoid and dwarf planet.

entities.name: Sedna
entities.description: A distant plutoid candidate possessing a highly eccentric orbit spanning thousands of Earth years.

entities.name: Moon
entities.description: The solitary natural satellite of planet Earth, orbiting at an average distance of roughly 384,400 kilometers.

entities.name: Phobos
entities.description: The larger and closer of the two natural satellites orbiting Mars.

entities.name: Deimos
entities.description: The smaller and more distant of the two natural satellites orbiting Mars.

entities.name: Io
entities.description: The innermost Galilean moon of Jupiter, known for extreme volcanic activity fueled by intense tidal heating.

entities.name: Europa
entities.description: A Galilean moon of Jupiter exhibiting a smooth icy surface covering a subterranean liquid water ocean.

entities.name: Ganymede
entities.description: The largest natural satellite in the Solar System, orbiting Jupiter and possessing its own magnetic field.

entities.name: Callisto
entities.description: The outermost Galilean moon of Jupiter, characterized by an ancient, heavily cratered surface.

entities.name: Titan
entities.description: The largest moon of Saturn, distinguished by a dense atmosphere and surface liquid hydrocarbon lakes.

entities.name: Enceladus
entities.description: A small icy satellite of Saturn featuring active cryovolcanic geysers venting from a sub-surface ocean.

entities.name: Triton
entities.description: The largest moon of Neptune, exhibiting a retrograde orbit that implies gravitational capture from the Kuiper Belt.

entities.name: Charon
entities.description: The largest natural satellite of Pluto, which mutually tidally locks with its parent dwarf planet.

entities.name: Hot Jupiter
entities.description: A category of giant exoplanets structurally comparable to Jupiter but maintaining short-period, close-in orbits near their host stars.

entities.name: Hot Neptune
entities.description: An exoplanet class similar to Neptune but situated in immediate proximity to its host star, driving high core densities and stripped envelopes.

entities.name: Cold Jupiter
entities.description: A gas giant exoplanet that orbits its parent star at a wide distance residing safely behind the system's frost line.

entities.name: Frost Line
entities.description: The radial boundary in a protoplanetary nebula where temperatures fall enough to allow volatile compounds to condense into solid grains.

entities.name: Nice Model
entities.description: A dynamic migration theory describing the late orbital rearrangement of the gas and ice giant planets within the early Solar System.

entities.name: Lunar Eclipse
entities.description: An alignment occurring when the Earth passes between the Moon and the Sun, placing the Moon completely or partially inside Earth's shadow.

entities.name: Solar Eclipse
entities.description: An alignment occurring when the Moon passes between the Earth and the Sun, blocking the Sun's light from the viewpoint of Earth.

entities.name: New Horizons
entities.description: A NASA space exploration probe designed to perform flyby investigations of the Pluto system and various outer Kuiper Belt targets.

entities.name: Tempel 1
entities.description: A short-period comet visited by NASA's Deep Impact mission, which released an impactor to study its interior composition.

entities.name: 25143 Itokawa
entities.description: A small near-Earth asteroid and the first asteroid from which JAXA's Hayabusa mission returned a surface sample to Earth.

entities.name: 486958 Arrokoth
entities.description: A cold classical Kuiper Belt object visited by New Horizons, found to be a contact binary formed by the gentle, low-speed merger of two separate bodies.

entities.name: Rosalind
entities.description: A small inner moon of Uranus discovered from Voyager 2 imagery.

entities.name: Belinda
entities.description: A small inner moon of Uranus discovered from Voyager 2 imagery.

entities.name: Puck
entities.description: The largest of Uranus's inner moons, discovered by Voyager 2 in 1985.

entities.name: 51 Pegasi
entities.description: A Sun-like star in the constellation Pegasus, notable as the host of the first exoplanet discovered orbiting a main-sequence star.

entities.name: 51 Pegasi b
entities.description: The first exoplanet discovered orbiting a Sun-like star, a gas giant on a short-period orbit that established the 'hot Jupiter' class.

entities.name: HD 209458
entities.description: A star hosting HD 209458 b, the first exoplanet observed transiting its host star.

entities.name: HD 209458 b
entities.description: A hot Jupiter exoplanet discovered via the transit method, notable as the first exoplanet confirmed to transit its host star.

entities.name: HL Tauri
entities.description: A very young star system whose protoplanetary disk was imaged by ALMA in radio wavelengths, revealing concentric gaps thought to be carved by forming planets.

entities.name: Beta Pictoris
entities.description: A young star system with a prominent debris disk and at least two known giant planets, used as a case study of ongoing planet formation.

entities.name: Tombaugh Regio
entities.description: A large heart-shaped bright region on Pluto's surface, imaged by New Horizons and named after Pluto's discoverer Clyde Tombaugh.

entities.name: Ahuna Mons
entities.description: A prominent cryovolcanic dome on Ceres, relatively young and uncratered compared to its surroundings.

entities.name: Kwanzaa Tholus
entities.description: A small domical mountain (tholus) on Ceres, believed to have degraded in height over time due to the weakness of its icy material.

entities.name: Great Red Spot
entities.description: A persistent high-pressure anticyclonic storm in Jupiter's atmosphere, the largest storm in the Solar System.

entities.name: Great Dark Spot
entities.description: A transient anticyclonic storm system observed in Neptune's atmosphere by Voyager 2.

entities.name: Tycho Crater
entities.description: A prominent, relatively young rayed impact crater on the Moon with a complex central peak structure.

entities.name: Cantaloupe Terrain
entities.description: A distinctive dimpled, cratered-melon-like terrain type found on the surface of Triton.

entities.name: Cassini
entities.description: A NASA/ESA/ASI orbiter that studied Saturn and its system of rings and moons for over a decade.

entities.name: Deep Impact
entities.description: A NASA mission that released an impactor into comet Tempel 1 to study the composition of its interior.

entities.name: Hayabusa
entities.description: A JAXA spacecraft that visited the near-Earth asteroid 25143 Itokawa and returned surface samples to Earth.

entities.name: MESSENGER
entities.description: A NASA spacecraft that orbited and mapped Mercury, confirming its rocky/metallic composition.

entities.name: Voyager 2
entities.description: A NASA probe and the only spacecraft to have flown by Uranus and Neptune, also responsible for close-up imagery of Triton.

entities.name: Juno
entities.description: A NASA orbiter studying Jupiter's atmosphere, interior, and polar storms using instruments including JunoCam, MWR, and JIRAM.

entities.name: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter
entities.description: A NASA orbiter that has mapped the Moon's surface in high resolution, including features such as Tycho Crater.

entities.name: Hubble Space Telescope
entities.description: A space-based optical/UV/near-infrared telescope used to image Solar System targets such as Jupiter's Great Red Spot and Uranus's moons and clouds.

entities.name: ALMA
entities.description: The Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array, a ground-based radio observatory that imaged the protoplanetary disk gaps around HL Tauri.

entities.name: Core Accretion Model
entities.description: A planet formation theory in which a rocky core grows through the collision and sticking of dust and planetesimals, eventually accreting a gaseous envelope if massive enough.

entities.name: Disk Instability Model
entities.description: A planet formation theory in which a local overdensity in a protoplanetary disk collapses directly under self-gravity to form a planet, characterized by the Toomre instability criterion.

hierarchy.title: Introduction to Planetary Astronomy and Heliophysics
